ddls:
CREATE TABLE "routes" (
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each route */,
  "start_name" TEXT /* Name of the starting point */,
  "end_name" TEXT /* Name of the ending point */,
  "start_point" INTEGER /* Identifier for the starting point */,
  "end_point" INTEGER /* Identifier for the ending point */,
  "google_distance" REAL /* Distance between start and end points measured by Google Maps */,
  "txt_distance" REAL /* Distance between start and end points measured by alternative method (.txt) */,
  "route_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the route type */,
  "estimated_travel_time" TEXT /* Estimated travel time for the route */,
  "actual_travel_time" TEXT /* Actual travel time for the route */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the route was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the route was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the route (e.g., active, inactive, planned) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or documentation for the route */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("route_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_routes_start_name FOREIGN KEY ("start_name") REFERENCES locations ("location_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_routes_end_name FOREIGN KEY ("end_name") REFERENCES locations ("location_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_routes_route_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("route_type_id") REFERENCES route_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the location */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the location */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "route_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each route type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the route type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the route type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "route_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change event */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* ID of the route that was changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change made */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value of the changed attribute */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the changed attribute */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_route_history_route_id FOREIGN KEY ("route_id") REFERENCES routes ("route_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_route_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., logistics manager, driver, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user last logged in */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, inactive, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "route_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* ID of the route being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the route */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the route was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the route was accessed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the route */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_route_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_route_access_route_id FOREIGN KEY ("route_id") REFERENCES routes ("route_id")
)
